description: A deep dive into English tenses specifically tailored for IELTS Speaking and Writing, including signal words, sentence structures, and over 60 contextual examples.

data.structure: Subject + V(s/es) | Subject + do/does not + V | Do/Does + Subject + V?
data.ielts_application: Used to state general facts, scientific truths (Writing Task 2), and permanent situations or habits (Speaking Part 1).

examples.sentence: Air pollution causes many respiratory diseases.
Technology plays an important role in modern life.
The Sun heats up the Earth's surface.
I usually go jogging every morning to keep fit.
Higher education provides students with specialized knowledge.
The school curriculum includes a wide range of subjects.
Digital devices are very popular among teenagers.
Sustainable farming practices protect biodiversity.
I work as a software developer for a multinational company.
Public transportation reduces carbon footprints.

data.ielts_application: Ideal for describing ongoing trends in Writing Task 1 or temporary actions in Speaking.

examples.sentence: The global temperature is rising at an alarming rate.
Many nations are investing in renewable energy infrastructure.
The city is developing quickly in terms of technology.
I am currently taking a vocational course in IT.
Scientists are conducting research on immunotherapy.
Consumers are increasingly choosing products with less packaging.
The number of students studying abroad is increasing.
People are becoming addicted to social media feeds.

data.ielts_application: Crucial for connecting past actions to the present. Used for life experiences and past events with current impacts.

examples.sentence: Human activity has caused irreversible damage to nature.
I have graduated from a prestigious university in the UK.
The government has introduced laws to curb carbon emissions.
Digital technology has changed how our brain processes information.
He has been a vegetarian since 1965.
Many species have faced extinction due to habitat loss.
We have participated in several volunteer activities this month.
The company has achieved its sales targets for this quarter.
I have already finished my assignment.
Technology has made our life easier.

data.structure: Subject + V2/ed | Subject + did not + V | Did + Subject + V?
data.ielts_application: Essential for Speaking Part 2 stories and Writing Task 1 descriptions of past data.

examples.sentence: I took a gap year before going to college.
The scandal ruined his promising career in politics.
The price of fossil fuels rose dramatically in 2020.
She aced the final exam because she studied very hard.
I saw an informative advertisement on TV yesterday.
They decided to go freelance after years in the corporate world.
The doctor examined the patient and gave some medicine.
The firm was wrongfully dismissed after the audit.
We visited the local market to buy fresh vegetables.
The invention of the internet revolutionized communication.

data.ielts_application: Used to set the scene for a story (Speaking Part 2) or describe actions in progress in the past.

examples.sentence: I was studying in the library when I found the research paper.
The wind was blowing strongly when we arrived.
He was working overtime while his colleagues were having a party.
I was looking for storybooks in the bookshop all afternoon.
They were discussing environmental issues during the meeting.
examples.note: Action in progress at a past point.

examples.sentence: The teacher was handing out the worksheets when the bell rang.
I was trying to keep fit by jogging in the park.
While I was browsing the internet, I saw a viral video.

data.ielts_application: Demonstrates logical sequence in narratives by showing which action happened first.

examples.sentence: By the time I reached the airport, the flight had departed.
He had finished his dissertation before the deadline was announced.
The oil had all been used up before they found a new source.
I realized I had forgotten to bring along my sunscreen.
They had established the company long before I joined the staff.
examples.note: Distant past compared to another past event.

examples.sentence: The students had studied up on the materials before the lecture.
As soon as he had passed muster, he received a scholarship.
She had already learned the ropes when she got the promotion.

data.structure: Subject + will + V | Subject + will not (won't) + V
data.ielts_application: Used for predictions, instant decisions, or expressing opinions about the future (Speaking Part 3).

examples.sentence: I think robots will replace manual workers in factories soon.
The new manager will give a presentation next Monday.
Technology will continue to impact education deeply.
I will try my best to reduce my carbon footprint.
Governments will introduce stricter laws to protect wildlife.
The Internet will be accessible in all remote areas.
If I am promoted, I will call the tune for the new project.
AI will likely transform the healthcare industry.

data.ielts_application: Essential for formal Writing (Task 1 Process or Task 2 arguments) to sound objective and academic.

examples.sentence: Radioactive waste must be disposed of safely.
Toxic chemicals were released into the river by the factory.
New software is being developed to improve data security.
Students are often required to learn long poems by heart.
Thousands of lives have been saved by medical technology.
